# Kuratchi Platform — API, MCP & SDK Reference

Project: Kuratchi Platform
Machine schema: /_assets/llms.json
Category: Cloud platform for managed databases, KV, and R2 storage on Cloudflare Workers

Two ways to interact with Kuratchi:
- MCP Server: Manage infrastructure from AI agents (create DBs, buckets, tokens)
- SDK (@kuratchi/sdk): Use infrastructure at runtime in deployed apps (query, read/write)

## 1) MCP Server (infrastructure management from AI agents)

Kuratchi exposes a Model Context Protocol server for full platform management.

### Setup

Add to your MCP client config (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "kuratchi": {
      "url": "https://mcp.kuratchi.cloud/sse",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer kdbp_your_token_here"
      }
    }
  }
}

### Available tools

- health: Check platform status
- list_databases / create_database / get_database / delete_database / redeploy_database
- query_database: Execute SQL against a database (requires dbName, sql, scoped token)
- list_kv_namespaces / create_kv_namespace / get_kv_namespace / delete_kv_namespace
- list_r2_buckets / create_r2_bucket / get_r2_bucket / delete_r2_bucket
- create_token: Create a scoped token for a resource (type, name, resource ID)
- revoke_token: Revoke a scoped token

## 2) SDK — @kuratchi/sdk (runtime usage in deployed apps)

The SDK is how your deployed application interacts with Kuratchi resources.
Use the MCP or dashboard to create resources, then use the SDK to access them.

### Install

npm install @kuratchi/sdk

### Initialize

import { createClient } from '@kuratchi/sdk';

const kuratchi = createClient({
  apiKey: process.env.KURATCHI_API_KEY,
  baseUrl: 'https://kuratchi.cloud',
});

### Database operations

const db = kuratchi.database('my-db');

// Parameterized query — returns rows as objects
const users = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE active = ?', [true]);

// DDL / write-only
await db.exec('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT)');

// Batch (transaction) — multiple statements atomically
await db.batch([
  { sql: 'INSERT INTO posts (title) VALUES (?)', params: ['Hello'] },
  { sql: 'INSERT INTO posts (title) VALUES (?)', params: ['World'] },
]);

// Raw mode — rows as arrays instead of objects
const raw = await db.raw('SELECT id, name FROM users');

// Drizzle ORM integration
import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-proxy';
const drizzleDb = drizzle(db.drizzleProxy());

### KV operations

const kv = kuratchi.kv('my-cache');

await kv.put('user:1', JSON.stringify({ name: 'Alice' }));
const value = await kv.get('user:1');
const keys = await kv.list({ prefix: 'user:' });
await kv.delete('user:1');

// With TTL and metadata
await kv.put('session:abc', data, {
  expirationTtl: 3600,
  metadata: { userId: '1' },
});
const { value, metadata } = await kv.getWithMetadata('session:abc');

### R2 operations

const r2 = kuratchi.r2('my-bucket');

// Upload
await r2.put('photos/cat.jpg', imageBuffer, { contentType: 'image/jpeg' });

// Download
const file = await r2.get('photos/cat.jpg');
// file.body: ReadableStream, file.etag, file.contentType, file.size

// List
const list = await r2.list({ prefix: 'photos/' });

// Delete
await r2.delete('photos/cat.jpg');

// Metadata only
const meta = await r2.head('photos/cat.jpg');

### Platform management (via SDK)

The SDK also exposes platform management (same as MCP, but programmatic).
Requires a platform token (kdbp_ prefix).

const kuratchi = createClient({ apiKey: 'kdbp_...', baseUrl: '...' });

// Databases
const dbs = await kuratchi.platform.databases.list();
await kuratchi.platform.databases.create({ name: 'my-db', locationHint: 'enam' });
await kuratchi.platform.databases.delete('db-id');

// KV
await kuratchi.platform.kv.create({ name: 'my-cache' });

// R2
await kuratchi.platform.r2.create({ name: 'my-bucket', locationHint: 'weur' });

// Scoped tokens
await kuratchi.platform.tokens.createForDatabase({ name: 'ci-token', databaseId: 'db-id' });
await kuratchi.platform.tokens.createForKv({ name: 'cache-token', kvNamespaceId: 'kv-id' });
await kuratchi.platform.tokens.createForR2({ name: 'storage-token', r2BucketId: 'r2-id' });
await kuratchi.platform.tokens.revoke('token-id');

## 3) REST API

Base URL: https://kuratchi.cloud/api/v1
Auth: Bearer token in Authorization header

### Token types

Platform token (kdbp_ prefix):
- Full management access — CRUD resources, manage tokens
- Obtain from dashboard: https://kuratchi.cloud/account/tokens

Scoped token:
- Access a single resource (one database, KV namespace, or R2 bucket)
- Create via API, MCP, or SDK using a platform token

### Databases

GET    /platform/databases          — List all databases
POST   /platform/databases          — Create database { name, locationHint? }
GET    /platform/databases/:id      — Get database details
DELETE /platform/databases/:id      — Delete database
PATCH  /platform/databases/:id      — Redeploy { action: "redeploy" }
POST   /:dbName                     — Query database { sql, params?, batch?, exec?, raw? }

### KV Namespaces

GET    /platform/kv                 — List all KV namespaces
POST   /platform/kv                 — Create namespace { name }
GET    /platform/kv/:id             — Get namespace details
DELETE /platform/kv/:id             — Delete namespace
POST   /kv/:kvName                  — KV operations { op: 'get'|'put'|'delete'|'list'|'getWithMetadata', key?, value?, ... }

### R2 Buckets

GET    /platform/r2                 — List all R2 buckets
POST   /platform/r2                 — Create bucket { name, locationHint? }
GET    /platform/r2/:id             — Get bucket details
DELETE /platform/r2/:id             — Delete bucket
GET    /r2/:r2Name                  — List objects
GET    /r2/:r2Name/:key             — Get object
PUT    /r2/:r2Name/:key             — Upload object
DELETE /r2/:r2Name/:key             — Delete object
HEAD   /r2/:r2Name/:key             — Get object metadata

### Tokens

POST   /platform/tokens             — Create scoped token { type, name, databaseId?, kvNamespaceId?, r2BucketId? }
DELETE /platform/tokens             — Revoke token { tokenId }

### AI Sessions

These endpoints are for application runtime use. They are REST endpoints, not MCP tools.
All AI endpoints require a platform token (kdbp_ prefix).

GET    /ai/sessions                  - List AI sessions for the authenticated organization
POST   /ai/sessions                  - Create a session { title?, model?, sessionId? }
GET    /ai/sessions/:sessionId       - Get session metadata plus current agent state
POST   /ai/chat                      - Send or resume chat { sessionId?, model?, message?, messages?, system?, reset? }

Notes:
- Sessions are organization-scoped
- `POST /ai/chat` creates or resumes a session
- `GET /ai/sessions` is the endpoint product apps should use to render resumable conversation history
- MCP remains infrastructure-only and does not expose chat/session tools

Create a session:

fetch('https://kuratchi.cloud/api/v1/ai/sessions', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'Authorization': 'Bearer kdbp_your_token_here'
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    title: 'Terminal copilot',
    model: '@cf/meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct-fp8-fast'
  })
});

Send a message:

fetch('https://kuratchi.cloud/api/v1/ai/chat', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'Authorization': 'Bearer kdbp_your_token_here'
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    sessionId: 'session-id',
    message: 'Explain why this command failed'
  })
});

List sessions:

fetch('https://kuratchi.cloud/api/v1/ai/sessions', {
  headers: {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer kdbp_your_token_here'
  }
});

## 4) End-to-end example for AI agents

Step 1: Get a platform token from https://kuratchi.cloud/account/tokens
Step 2: Add the MCP server to your config (see section 1)
Step 3: Use MCP tools to set up infrastructure:

  1. create_database(name: "my-app-db")
  2. create_token(type: "database", name: "my-app-token", databaseId: "<id>")

Step 4: Install the SDK in your project and use it at runtime:

  npm install @kuratchi/sdk

  import { createClient } from '@kuratchi/sdk';
  const kuratchi = createClient({
    apiKey: '<scoped token from step 3>',
    baseUrl: 'https://kuratchi.cloud',
  });
  const db = kuratchi.database('my-app-db');
  await db.exec('CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT)');
  await db.query('INSERT INTO users VALUES (?, ?)', ['1', 'Alice']);
  const users = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users');

## 5) Location hints

When creating databases or R2 buckets, you can pass a locationHint:
- wnam: Western North America
- enam: Eastern North America
- weur: Western Europe
- eeur: Eastern Europe
- apac: Asia Pacific

If omitted, Cloudflare selects automatically based on proximity.
